summary: Ingest PRED-106 (16 Hz Ca²⁺ ICR) into data/predictions.json — assessment ready; phantom WIN-071 citation flagged; window closes 2026-04-30
detail: PRED-106 (16 Hz Aetheric Field Increases Ca²⁺ Signaling >20%) was registered on the dome's predictions.html on 2026-04-04 (SHA256 1a364b8325dd409bb5ae806b43b7ab3523a144ed60ff718945988e3c27dc30c1) and has been carried by the poller as 'changes_pending_analysis = 1' for ~25 days. Analyst has now written a Mode-1b assessment with full suggested_entry at monitor/analyst/expansions/PRED-assessment-PRED-106.json. Decider actions: (1) Add the suggested_entry to data/predictions.json's entries[] array (it is structurally complete and mirrors PRED-105's ingestion schema). (2) Set our_verdict='pending' and re-assign for verdict update on first post-closure poll (2026-05-01 morning or evening of 2026-04-30 EOM, whichever lands first). (3) Note the phantom WIN-071 citation: PRED-106's stated empirical basis is 'WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation' but the dome's published wins.html only lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 (verified 2026-04-29 via curl, 951 lines). WIN-071 does NOT exist in the public registry. This is recorded in the assessment's phantom_citation_flag field for downstream tracking and may justify a separate analyst sweep across all PRED-### entries to surface other internal-consistency gaps. (4) Tighten the test_window_closes string to '2026-04-30' rather than the imprecise 'April 2026' so Step 10b imminent-window detection works cleanly. (5) Apply post-closure rubric on next assessment: silent drop → silent_ignore (mirrors PRED-105 trajectory); pass-mark via citing pre-existing Liboff/Blackman literature → recycled / standard_physics-with-disputed-mechanism; genuine pre-registered replication with N, α, blinded counts → assess on merit.
